sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 15g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a tuber, this mostly lives or dies on carb behaviour.
sections.subtitleText: 15g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a tuber, this mostly lives or dies on carb behaviour.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 41g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.
sections.subtitleText: 41g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 3.4g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.
sections.subtitleText: 3.4g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: potassium at 13% daily value stands out most. useful, but still pretty limited.
sections.subtitleText: potassium at 13% daily value stands out most. useful, but still pretty limited.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: pros first: strong comfort-food appeal because a very real reason people like them, even if the nutrition fit is weak. still retains some tuber identity because not nutritionally empty, just much worse than stronger tuber forms. satisfying texture and taste because part of the real-world appeal, especially for content framing.

sections.subtitleText: pros first: strong comfort-food appeal because a very real reason people like them, even if the nutrition fit is weak. still retains some tuber identity because not nutritionally empty, just much worse than stronger tuber forms. satisfying texture and taste because part of the real-world appeal, especially for content framing.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides: fried format is a major category negative because this drags tubers away from the cleaner energy-food role the category is supposed to reward. glycemic behaviour is rough because a major practical strike against the food in a carb-behaviour category. energy density is much harsher than ideal because much easier to overshoot calories than with cleaner tuber forms.

closing.summary: Fries does have a few real-world upsides. but the nutrition case is still weak and the downsides drag it down hard. best only when you want easy carbs and can accept the missing upside.
closing.useCaseNote: Best only when you want easy carbs and can accept the missing upside.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in D tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in D tier with an overall score of 29 from a base nutrition score of 35. Context dragged it down by 6 points.
